{"step_by_step_thinking": "Zinc oxide eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement are both used in dentistry. Polycarboxylate cement is known for its chemical bond to tooth structure, which is a characteristic feature of this type of cement. However, there is no information suggesting that zinc oxide eugenol cement forms a chemical bond to tooth structure in the same way. Chelation is a process that occurs in glass ionomer cements, not in zinc oxide eugenol cement. There is no mention of eugenol being substituted by EBA in zinc oxide eugenol cement. Polyacrylic acid is a component of polycarboxylate cement, but it is not mentioned as a liquid in the context of zinc oxide eugenol cement.", "answer_choice": "B"}
